ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Min Jiang Kueh; 'pancake food product' (FOODON_03315571) is the closest defensible parent class. Coconut-filled variant is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (Pancakes and similar products) is the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for Min Jiang Kueh in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Min Jiang Kueh or its direct parent category in the clinical terminology system.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Min Jiang Kueh (coconut) weighs approximately 100 g and is typically consumed as a single snack or breakfast item.

nutrient_source.title: Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut), CR ID F11090035. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Pancake, made from packet mix — GI value 67 (analogous estimate for wheat-flour pancake base)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) in the University of Sydney GI Database. The closest defensible analogue is a wheat-flour pancake (GI ~67, medium category) from the University of Sydney GI Database. The coconut-sugar filling does not materially lower the glycaemic response of the refined wheat flour base. GL per serving calculated as: (67 × 34.82 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 100 g serving) / 100 = 23.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Borderline sodium (237 mg/100 g) with low potassium (90 mg/100 g) and insufficient dietary fibre (2.31 g/100 g) to offset sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) to occasional small portions, as its sodium content of 237 mg/100 g sits in the borderline range and is not offset by adequate potassium or fibre; the added sugar and coconut saturated fat further reduce its suitability for regular dietary inclusion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load (34.82 g/100 g) from a refined wheat flour base with medium-to-high GI (~67), low dietary fibre (2.31 g/100 g), and added sugar (8.61 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) due to its high refined-carbohydrate content, medium GI of approximately 67, and glycaemic load of 23 per 100 g serving, which will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion size should be strictly limited and paired with low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Coconut-derived saturated fat (~3.2 g/100 g), added sugar (8.61 g/100 g), and borderline sodium (237 mg/100 g) without compensating unsaturated fats or protective micronutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should consume Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) sparingly, as its saturated fat content from coconut (~3.2 g/100 g), added sugar, and borderline sodium collectively contribute to an unfavourable cardiovascular risk profile; it should not be a regular dietary item.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coconut-derived saturated fat (~3.2 g/100 g) and added sugar (8.61 g/100 g) with no qualifying anti-inflammatory factors
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) as its coconut saturated fat does not qualify for the MUFA/PUFA anti-inflammatory carve-out, its added sugar promotes systemic inflammation, and no positive anti-inflammatory factor (omega-3, polyphenols, whole-food fibre >3 g, fermented food, or anti-inflammatory spice) is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) is a traditional Singaporean street snack and breakfast item consisting of a thick, soft, leavened wheat-flour pancake folded around a filling of finely grated or desiccated coconut mixed with sugar. The pancake batter is made from wheat flour, egg, sugar, baking powder, and water, then pan-fried on a flat griddle until golden and spongy. The coconut filling provides a moist, slightly chewy texture with a sweet, tropical flavour that contrasts with the soft, slightly crisp pancake exterior. Min Jiang Kueh is commonly sold at hawker centres, wet markets, and pasar malam (night markets) across Singapore and Malaysia, and is closely associated with Malay and Peranakan food culture. Nutritionally, each 100 g serving provides 194 kcal, 34.82 g carbohydrate, 4.09 g protein, 4.25 g fat (of which approximately 3.2 g is coconut-derived saturated fat), 8.61 g sugar, 2.31 g dietary fibre, and 237 mg sodium. The refined wheat flour base gives the food a medium glycaemic index of approximately 67 and a glycaemic load of approximately 23 per 100 g, making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The coconut filling adds saturated fat without providing compensating unsaturated fats or anti-inflammatory nutrients, which is a concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management. The food is vegetarian, halal, and dairy-free but contains gluten and egg allergens.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11090035) at confidence 0.95. The food is an exact match in the primary regional source. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (wheat-flour pancake, University of Sydney GI Database) and clearly documented as an analogous estimate. Saturated fat and potassium values are estimated from ingredient composition consistent with HPB data and documented accordingly.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut) are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F11090035, and USDA FoodData Central does not contain a matching entry for this specific traditional Singaporean preparation as of 2026-04-16; these fields cannot be populated from any approved source without fabrication. saturated_fat_g (~3.2 g/100 g) is estimated from the coconut filling ingredient composition consistent with the HPB total fat value of 4.25 g/100 g; this is an approximation and should be verified against a detailed fatty acid analysis. potassium_mg (90 mg/100 g) is estimated from ingredient composition (wheat flour + coconut) using USDA FoodData Central reference values and is not directly reported in the HPB entry. GI value of 67 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour pancake from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut). All core macros (protein_g 4.09, fat_g 4.25, carbohydrate_g 34.82) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the precise canonical English name 'Min Jiang Kueh (Coconut)'. All five mandatory Singaporean-dish languages are present: English (food_name), Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (194.0), protein_g (4.09), fat_g (4.25), carbohydrate_g (34.82) are all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (CR ID F11090035, confidence 0.95). nutrient_source is fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (2.31), sodium_mg (237.0), saturated_fat_g (3.2), sugar_g (8.61) are all present as numeric values. saturated_fat_g is an ingredient-based estimate documented in notes.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (67), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (23.3), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (wheat-flour pancake), clearly documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03315571 (pancake food product, closest_parent match) and FoodEx2 A0EJX (Pancakes and similar products, closest_parent match) are defensible and honest. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes are invented.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes'.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Updated ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with scoring model allowed values.

Iteration 3: Expanded notes field to explicitly document the missing-data basis for every -1 sentinel value in per_100g, citing HPB F11090035 and USDA FoodData Central as the consulted approved sources that do not report these fields for this specific preparation, resolving g3_numeric_hygiene failure from validator feedback.

The g3_numeric_hygiene failure from iteration 2 has been resolved by explicitly documenting in the notes field the missing-data basis and approved sources consulted for every -1 sentinel value across monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, all vitamin fields, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g.
